one_line_ask: Grade four raw property photos, vectorize the Marlow & Vane crest, source a subtle linen texture, then run the client's authored InDesign flyer template through a data-merge against a four-listing roster CSV to batch-produce a print-ready PDF and per-listing high-res JPEGs.
brief: Marlow & Vane Realty, a boutique residential agency, needs its new-listing flyer sheet finished for print and email. Our marketing lead has handed off an authored InDesign flyer template (listing_flyer_template.indd) — a US Letter portrait one-listing-per-page layout in a clean editorial style with a hero photo frame, three thumbnail frames, a crest placement, a full-bleed background frame for a subtle paper/linen texture, and REAL data-merge fields (<<address>>, <<price>>, <<beds>>, <<baths>>, <<sqft>>, <<blurb>>, <<agent_name>>, <<agent_phone>>). We are also handing off a listings roster CSV (four current new listings, one row each with those exact field names), four raw agent-shot property photos (a curb-appeal exterior, a bright living room, a kitchen, a primary bedroom), and the agency crest as a flat PNG. The photos are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, dull/flat, cool white-balance, and slightly under- or over-exposed by window light. The job: (1) make every property photo look bright, warm and inviting — straighten, auto-tone, warm the white balance toward a welcoming daylight look, gently lift shadows, push vibrance a touch (never garish — this is real estate, not a b
